text: Fill in the blanks about endocrine physiology.
questions: The hypothalamus controls the anterior pituitary via *releasing/inhibiting* hormones transported through the hypothalamo-hypophyseal *portal* system.
Thyroid hormones T3 and T4 are synthesised from *tyrosine* residues on thyroglobulin and require *iodine* for their production.
In Graves' disease, thyroid-stimulating *antibodies/immunoglobulins* bind to TSH receptors, causing hyperthyroidism with a suppressed *TSH* level.
Insulin is secreted by *beta* cells of the pancreatic islets and promotes *glucose uptake* by cells, lowering blood sugar.
Cortisol is secreted by the zona *fasciculata* of the adrenal cortex and is regulated by the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (*HPA*) axis.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
